verdict: All four pieces meet the standard. Post 1 (market sophistication) is the strongest pillar post in the set. Posts 2 and 3 carry the framework without overlapping. The newsletter extends meaningfully with the identification layer and owner type mapping that didn't appear in the posts.
strengths: Post 1 opens with a claim that makes the reader re-examine their own pitch — sharp hook that works for the target operator
The before/after mechanism examples in Post 3 are specific enough to be immediately adoptable, not illustrative filler
The awareness ladder diagnostic question in Post 2 is concrete and repeatable — operators can use it tomorrow
Newsletter extends the posts with identification and the owner type matrix, genuinely adding value rather than recapping what was already said
Owner type mapping in the newsletter is type-specific and accurate — Steward as identification buyer, Investor as mechanism buyer, each treated distinctly
problems: Post 2 runs close to 2,900 characters — verify final character count before publishing

Post 3's closing 'building your mechanism statements' section is useful but slightly more instructional in tone than the rest of the series — may benefit from one concrete example added before the closing test question
required_fixes: Verify post_2.txt character count is under 2,900 including CTA footer
Verify post_3.txt character count is under 2,900 including CTA footer
